title: Open-Air Cinema in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin's open-air cinema season (Freiluftkino) runs from early May to around mid-September.** Films start around dusk, which in June and July usually means between 21:00 and 21:30, so plan to eat first and treat the film as the second half of the evening.

**To watch in English, learn the listing codes.** OV is the original language, which for most big films means English. OmU is the original language with German subtitles, so still English audio. OmeU adds English subtitles. A plain German dub usually has no code at all.

**Filter for OV or OmU on the cinema's own website**, or use the citywide list at openair-kino.net. Check the code next to the specific date, because the same film can screen dubbed one night and in English the next.

**Central and easy:** Kino Central in the Haus Schwarzenberg courtyard is two minutes from Hackescher Markt, and Sommerkino Kulturforum sets up deckchairs with the skyline behind the screen near Potsdamer Platz.

**Big park screens with English filters:** Freiluftkino Friedrichshain, Freiluftkino Rehberge (U6 Rehberge drops you at the gate) and Freiluftkino Kreuzberg. Freiluftkino Hasenheide plays come rain or shine and suits families.

**Bring a layer, book popular nights ahead** (tickets are between about 8.50 and 12 euros depending on the venue), arrive 20 to 30 minutes early for a seat, and check the venue's weather policy before you travel.
